mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real sales of goods and services exceeding 0.5% signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or signal loses predictive power during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate indicates labor market tightness that fuels wage inflation, prompting aggressive monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5%, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production rising for three consecutive months confirms supply-side recovery that alleviates cost pressures, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered months, or the signal reverses direction during monetary tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between CPIAUCSL growth and the federal funds rate signals real rate compression that forces central banks to accelerate hiking, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal predicts positive returns in >50% of triggered instances, or interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts reflect weakening long-term demand expectations and tighter credit conditions, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where HOUST declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market cooling that reduces inflationary pressures and allows monetary policy stabilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered months yield negative 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or signal fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5% concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP confirms economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered quarters, or signal loses predictive power during liquidity injection periods.
